product_direction: Public GitHub registry of AI-tool-neutral skills that any AI agent installs/updates by reading the repo protocol — no clone, no scripts, no symlinks.

features.title: Repo bootstrap: git init, harness scaffold, spec committed, GitHub private->public repo created (confirm repo name first)
Migrate & convert skills per spec §7 (except worklog, update-tags): frontmatter + standard footer + neutrality pass
Write README protocol (INSTALL/UPDATE for AI agents, verbatim publish-line, verify checklists)
Registry generator + first registry.json; acceptance: real UC1 install on this machine with Claude Code
Provenance schema + full UPDATE protocol (commit-log preview, local_modified 3-way ask)
CONTRIBUTING.md (review checklist, Co-Authored-By rule, markdown-only policy) + branch protection
CI validate.yml: registry sync, frontmatter/footer, no personal paths, no executables
skill-publisher meta-skill (ask share/publish, audit, normalize, regenerate registry, push/PR)
Generalize worklog (split personal Notion config from logic) and publish
Test matrix: UC1-UC4 on both Claude Code and Codex, results recorded in harness/notes

Decide xlsx-safe-export disposition (deferred from AIS-002): its core is a Python validator, which §6.2 markdown-only forbids. Options: keep local-only like update-tags; add a maintainer-tooling carve-out to §6.2; or ship an advisory markdown-only version. Requires a spec decision before any migration.

skill-updater meta-skill (base UPDATE/UC2 flow as an installable skill): inventory provenance, check upstream via host API (no clone), preview path-scoped commit log, confirm-then-apply, respect local_modified 3-way. Landed via PR #1 (d32eb39); backfilled to harness post-merge.

skill-updater enhancement: discover skills new in the registry — fetch registry index, report entries with no local folder, install only on confirmation (closes the new-skill blind spot). Landed via PR #2 (9ff443e); backfilled to harness post-merge.

Prevent skill↔harness drift — Layer 2: init.sh parity guard. WARN if any skills/<name> is not referenced anywhere in harness/ (word-boundary grep across feature_list.json + progress.md + notes). Local, no push. Backstop that catches drift from any publish path (incl. external PRs) at next session start.

Prevent skill↔harness drift — Layer 1: teach skill-publisher to update the project's work-control memory when the target repo has one (guarded/tool-neutral: skip if no harness). Closes the self-publish path at source. Republished to main via PR #3 (rebase-merge, commit 4b349d0).
Prevent skill↔harness drift — Layer 3: CONTRIBUTING note that maintainers record merged skills in harness/ (contributors don't touch it); ties to the init.sh parity warning.

Add LICENSE (MIT, © 2026 Auttapong Tura). A public install/contribute registry with no license = full copyright by default, which forbids the reuse the INSTALL protocol depends on. Root LICENSE file covers all markdown skills; inbound=outbound for contributions.

README sync: add a License section; add the 'discover skills new in the registry' step to the UPDATE protocol (align with skill-updater/AIS-013); note that INSTALL/UPDATE/publish flows are also installable as meta-skills (skill-updater, skill-publisher).
